title: Cast iron cleaning paste
subtitle: A coarse salt and oil paste for scrubbing stuck food from cast iron without stripping the seasoning
excerpt: Soap strips the seasoned surface of cast iron. Coarse salt used with a small amount of oil removes stuck-on food by abrasion without the alkalinity that damages the seasoning layer. This paste is made fresh at the sink before each use. The ratio is simple: a tablespoon of salt to a teaspoon of oil.
sourceNotes: Cast iron care: Lodge Cast Iron care guide (2023). Salt abrasion method: coarse sea salt or kosher salt acts as a physical abrasive. Oil prevents the salt from scratching the pan metal and helps lift residue. Method suitable for stuck-on food; not for rust removal (which requires more intensive re-seasoning).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh each use; do not mix salt and oil in advance.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Make fresh each use. Mixed salt and oil degrades if stored. Keep salt and oil separately on the shelf.

glossaryTerms.definition: The polymerised oil layer built up on the cooking surface of a cast iron pan through repeated high-heat oiling. It forms a non-stick surface and prevents rust. Alkaline cleaners such as dish soap strip this layer.
Using a gritty or textured material to physically lift residue from a surface without chemical action. Coarse salt is a food-safe abrasive that dissolves in the final rinse.

content.text: is an investment: years of use build up a smooth, resilient, non-stick surface. Dish soap is alkaline and strips this layer. The alternative for stuck-on food is
with coarse salt and a small amount of oil. The salt lifts residue by physical scrubbing; the oil stops the metal from drying out during the process. Rinse and dry immediately after cleaning.

items.prepNote: About 1 tablespoon; kosher salt also works well
About 1 teaspoon; any cooking oil; combine with salt just before use

content.text: While the pan is still warm (not hot), tip the coarse salt into it. Add the oil and work the mixture around the pan with a stiff brush or folded paper towel. Scrub stuck areas in a circular motion. Pour off the salt paste, rinse with warm (not cold) water, and dry the pan immediately over low heat or with a cloth. Apply a thin wipe of oil to the clean dry surface before storing.
Keep coarse salt and oil in the kitchen in their normal containers. Make this paste fresh at each use; do not store mixed salt and oil.
